definition: The Fed's effective Taylor coefficient has collapsed to ~1.0-1.2, structurally constrained by fiscal feedback. At $36T+ debt, every 100bp rate increase adds ~$250-320B in annual interest expense, partially recycled into demand via the Kalecki channel (10-25% offset to tightening intent). This compresses the Fed's effective toolkit bandwidth to ~155-240bp from ~500bp+ pre-pandemic.
evidence: Fed paused at 5.25-5.50% with core PCE at 4.3%, then cut to 4.25-4.50% with core PCE at 2.7%
Implied asymmetric phi_pi of ~1.2-1.3 on tightening, ~0.8-1.0 on easing
Goldman FCI eased ~100bp equivalent despite 525bp of hikes
Convergence across incompatible NK and PK frameworks in pair_2 debate raises confidence to 8/10
open_questions: Is the Taylor coefficient collapse permanent or could it reverse with lower debt/GDP?
What is the minimum effective bandwidth of the Fed toolkit under current fiscal conditions?
Could unconventional tools (YCC, targeted facilities) expand effective bandwidth?
